headline: Wealthfront Prices IPO at $14/Share on Nasdaq, Raises ~$300M With Goldman and JPMorgan as Lead Underwriters
the_so_what: Wealthfront listing on Nasdaq with Goldman and JPMorgan as lead underwriters validates the public market appetite for AI-native wealth platforms at a time when traditional wealth firms face valuation pressure from the Altruist-triggered selloff. The absence of disclosed AUM or revenue in the pricing announcement suggests the market is valuing Wealthfront on growth trajectory and platform economics rather than current assets — the same playbook Robinhood used in its 2021 IPO.
summary: Wealthfront priced its initial public offering at $14 per share on the Nasdaq Global Select Market (WLTH), with 34.6 million shares offered for a total offering value of approximately $484 million. The company raised ~$300 million from its own shares, with Goldman Sachs and J.P. Morgan as lead underwriters alongside Citigroup, Wells Fargo Securities, and RBC Capital Markets. Wealthfront describes itself as a tech-driven financial platform for digital natives, though no AUM or revenue figures were disclosed in the pricing announcement.

key_stat.label: Raised in Wealthfront IPO on Nasdaq (December 2025) — ticker: WLTH

verified_claims: IPO priced at $14.00 per share — top of $12–$14 range (GlobeNewswire official press release December 12, 2025)
Began trading on Nasdaq Global Select Market as WLTH on December 12, 2025 (press release)
21,538,461 shares offered by the company (press release)
Gross proceeds to Wealthfront approximately $300.6M (press release)
Total proceeds including selling stockholders: $484.6M (press release)
Goldman Sachs and J.P. Morgan as lead underwriters (press release)
30-day greenshoe option for up to 5,192,308 additional shares (press release)
_governance.notes: All claims sourced directly from GlobeNewswire official press release dated December 12, 2025. human_approved: Track 2 entry written directly from verified source.
